title: Revenue Operations Manager (internal promotion at Ledgerly)
fitRationale: You already carry lead responsibilities at Ledgerly: you were promoted to Senior Analyst with team-lead scope, ran a three-person workstream, mentored two junior analysts, and cut forecast variance from 18% to 7%. Because you said you want more ownership and to manage people, formalizing that into a manager role inside Ledgerly is the most direct next step.
upskills: Run regular 1:1s and performance reviews (take a short people-management course or internal mentorship to learn calibration and feedback cadence).
Ownership of hiring and headcount planning (build a 6–12 month hiring plan for your workstream).
Operationalize commission governance and escalation (translate your SQL commission engine into a documented, auditable process).
firstMove: This month: create a one-page 'manager proposal' summarizing outcomes (forecast variance drop, commission engine, exec dashboard) and ask your manager for a 30-minute meeting to request a formal manager title/headcount and outline the role you'd take.

ambitionCheck.note: You already lead a three-person workstream and have informal mentoring experience—formal management is a credible next step if you convert that informal scope into documented team outcomes.

title: Principal Revenue Operations — Systems & Analytics Lead (senior IC at Ledgerly)
fitRationale: You built Ledgerly’s first SQL-based commission calculation engine, own the executive revenue dashboard, and redesigned the forecasting model (variance 18% → 7%). If you prefer to keep shipping technical systems and own platform-level reliability rather than people management, this role matches your demonstrated technical impact and desire for more ownership.
upskills: Productionize the SQL commission engine: add tests, CI, rollback plans and documentation for handoffs.
Advance dashboard governance: create dashboard SLAs, data lineage docs (NetSuite → Salesforce → Tableau).
Deepen data-platform collaboration: own API integrations and coordinate with engineering/BI on scale and latency.
firstMove: This month: perform a two-week audit of the commission engine and executive dashboard, produce a short roadmap with risks and SLOs, and present it to the RevOps director as a proposal to formalize a systems-lead IC role.

ambitionCheck.note: You already shipped the commission engine and executive dashboard; pushing those artifacts to production-grade is a natural, supported step rather than a stretch.

title: Sales Finance / Revenue Finance Manager (internal rotation to GTM FP&A at Ledgerly)
fitRationale: You have strong adjacent finance skills used day-to-day at Ledgerly—forecasting, NetSuite, commission design and financial modeling—plus stakeholder management in a fintech/SaaS GTM context. An internal move to sales finance leverages your domain and gives the manager-level ownership you want while keeping you at the company.
upskills: Close the loop on month-end finance processes (shadow FP&A and own a month-close deliverable).
Tighten financial-storytelling for execs (build clear P&L narratives tied to forecast variances).
Formalize accounting/ERP knowledge (deeper NetSuite admin skills or a short finance certificate).
firstMove: This month: schedule a shadow day with the FP&A lead, bring a cleaned version of your forecast and ask to own one forecasting cadence item for the next close to demonstrate fit.

ambitionCheck.note: You already run forecasting and use NetSuite and commission design—those are the core skills FP&A needs, so an internal rotation to sales finance is realistic with a few tactical proofs of capability.
